pages.html:
From The Founder's Desk

Dr. Patangrao Kadam

Founder, Bharati Vidyapeeth Deemed University

I am indeed happy to welcome you to Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Institute of Management & Information Technology, Navi Mumbai, which has a large pool of new executive talent waiting to step into the Corporate World.

I established Bharati Vidyapeeth in 1964 with a realization that all-round social transformation can be brought about through the spread of education. During the last 55 years, we have established more than 156 educational units of various kinds, right from Pre-Primary School to a full-fledged University, in different parts of India and even in the capital city New Delhi. Bharati Vidyapeeth is an institution with its distinct ideology, which can be briefly stated thus. Our objective is: “To make quality education accessible to the students coming from different regions and different strata of the Society and to provide the same to them as per their individual choices and inclination at affordable prices”. Our constant endeavor is to create ambience in the campuses propitious for learning by providing continuously updated courses and with the help of enriched infrastructural facilities such as well equipped laboratories, rich library and well maintained playground, hostels. It is a gratifying experience for me to know that thousands of students who have passed out from the portals of our institutions have established themselves very well in different walks of life.

In April 1996, the Government of India conferred the status of University to a cluster of our institutions in accordance with the provision in the University Grants Commission Act, 1956. Since the inception of this University, our endeavor has been to develop it as a lively and throbbing centre of learning and research. I am very happy to say that, within the short span , this University has established its academic reputation not only across the country but also overseas. This is evident from the fact that we get students from different parts of India and also from number of countries across the globe. I have no doubt that we will be able to achieve theses objectives in co-operation with our faculties of various institutions which include experts, experienced and caring teachers.

I wish, your association with BVIMIT, Navi Mumbai, would be mutually beneficial one, which can be le veraged to yield promising results for both of us. The institute has a flagship Post-graduate programme, viz., Master of Computer Applications (MCA) affiliated to Mumbai University, approved by AICTE and NBA Accredited till June 2022.

We are confident you will find here, the perfect profile to complem ent your requirements.

From The Principal's Desk

Dr. Suhasini Vijaykumar

M.C.A, Ph. D.
Principal, BVIMIT

I would like to extend my sincere gratitude to each and every one of our stakeholders – our corporate and academic partners, alumni, faculty and students who have believed in us and supported us through our journey of becoming a successful technical and management institute over the past decade.

Meeting the challenging IT requirements of the corporates is the prime responsibility of software engineers, BVIMIT, spares no effort to make its curriculum meaningful addressing the requirement. A team of dedicated faculty with a judicious blend of academic and industry background has made the delivery of the MCA programme most effective to end users.

The Master of Computer Applications (MCA) programme is affiliated to University of Mumbai and NBA Accredited till June 2022. The course is approved by AICTE and enables the students to comprehend the concepts and functional aspects of Information Technology.

Our alumni have made their mark in various fields and their achievements stand as a testimony to our endeavour and their hard work. Our students are equipped with the necessary skills that creates successful IT managers and entrepreneurs.

Vision

Providing high quality, innovative and value-based education in information technology to build competent professionals.

Mission

M1. Technical Skills:- To provide solid technical foundation theoretically as well as Practically capable of providing quality services to industry.

M2. Development:-Department caters to the needs of students through comprehensive educational programs and promotes life long learning in the field of computer Applications.

M3. Ethical leadership:-Department develops ethical leadership insight in the students to succeed in industry, government and academia.

pages.html:
Master of Computer Application NBA Accredited
  • Master of Computer Applications (MCA)is a two-years (four semesters) professional Master's Degree in computer science awarded in India. The course was designed to meet the growing demand for qualified professionals in the field of Information Technology. It is a postgraduate course that can be taken up after obtaining a Bachelor's Degree. The MCA programme is inclined more toward Application Development and thus has more emphasis on latest programming language and tools to develop better and faster applications.
  • MCA was introduced in BVIMIT in the year 2002. It is two year full time programme affiliated to the Mumbai University approved by AICTE with the intake of 120 students. Spanning over four semesters, the course not only includes subjects that give a good grounding in the IT concepts, but also includes management oriented papers like ‘IT of Management, etc, thus preparing the student to be more competent to face the dynamic corporate world.
Mission & Vision
  • Providing high quality, innovative and value-based education in information technology to build competent professionals.
  • Mission
    • M1. Technical Skills:--To provide solid technical foundation theoretically as well as Practically capable of providing quality services to multi-disciplinary industry.
    • M2. Development: -To provide comprehensive educational programs and promote lifelong learning in the field of Computer Applications based on the changing needs of IT industry and society worldwide.
    • M3.Innovation:-To promote innovation and excellence in delivery and assessment to ensure ethical and holistic development of students for employability, entrepreneurship and higher education.
      • M4.Ethical leadership: - To develop ethical leadership awareness in the students to succeed in industry, government and academia.
Programme Educational Objectives (PEOs)
PEO1: Practice software engineering approaches to solve real world concerns and develop software to meet users requirements in a multi-disciplinary environment .
PEO2: Excel in professional career and/or higher education by acquiring knowledge in mathematical, statistics, management and computer applications.
PEO3: To nurture the creative, innovative and entrepreneurial potential of students for employment and lifelong learning
PEO4: To create competent Professionals with social and ethical attitude, communication skills, leadership qualities, team work
Programme Outcomes
PO 1 Computational Knowledge: An ability to apply knowledge of computing, mathematics, science and application design and development fundamentals appropriate to the discipline.
PO 2 Problem Analysis: An ability to identify, conduct literature survey and formulate the computing requirements appropriate to its solution.
PO 3 Design / Development of Solutions: Design and evaluate solutions for complex problems, components or processes that meet specified needs considering public health and safety, cultural, societal, and environmental parameters.
PO 4 Conduct investigations of complex Computing problems: Use research-based knowledge, analyze and interpret data by applying appropriate research methods, tools and design experiments.
PO 5 Modern Tool Usage: Create, select, adapt and apply appropriate techniques, skills, resources and modern IT tools necessary for computing activities with an understanding of the limitations.
PO 6 Professional Ethics: Understand and commit to professional ethics and cyber regulations, responsibilities, and norms of professional computing practices
PO 7 Life-long Learning: Recognize the need and engage in lifelong learning independently for continual development to improve knowledge and competence as a computing professional
PO 8 Project management and finance: An understanding of IT and management principles and apply to multidisciplinary software development, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects considering economical and financial factors.
PO 9 Communication Efficacy: Understand and communicate effectively with the computing audiences and with society at large, about complex computing systems activities confidently and effectively by writing effective reports and design documentations.
PO 10 Societal and Environmental Concern: Understand and assess societal, environmental, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues within local and global contexts, and the consequential responsibilities relevant to professional computing practices.
PO 11 Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, as a member or leader in diverse teams in multidisciplinary environments.
PO 12 Innovation and Entrepreneurship: Recognize timely opportunity and innovate to be an entrepreneur to pursue and create value addition for the betterment of the individual and society at large.
PSO 1 Provide effective and efficient real time solutions using acquired knowledge in various domains.
PSO 2 Apply standard practices of software project development for desktop, web, mobile and cross platform applications using modern tools and technologies, encouraging open-source programming environments.
Programme Contents
  • MCA11 Mathematical Foundation for Computer Science 1
  • MCA12 Advanced Java
  • MCA13 Advanced Database Management System
  • MCA14 Software Project Management
  • MCAL11 Data Structure Lab
  • MCAL12 Advanced Java LAB
  • MCAL13 Advanced Database Management System LAB
  • MCAL14 Web Technologies
  • MCAP11 Mini Project – 1 A
  • MCA21 Mathematical Foundation for Computer Science 2
  • MCA22 Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
  • MCA23 Information Security
  • MCAE24 Elective - 1
    • MCAE241 Image Processing
    • MCAE242 Internet Of Things
    • MCAE243 Robotic Process Automation
    • MCAE244 Computer Vision
    • MCAE245 Embedded Systems
  • MCAE25 Elective - 2
    • MCAE251 Natural Language Processing
    • MCAE252 Geographic Information System
    • MCAE253 Design and Analysis of Algorithm
    • MCAE254 Digital Marketing and Business Analytics
    • MCAE255 Research Methodology
  • MCAL21 Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Lab
  • MCAL22 Soft Skill Development Lab
  • MCALE23 Elective 1 Lab
  • MCAL24 Skill based Lab Course AWT Lab
  • MCAL25 Skill based Lab Course User Interface Lab
  • MCAL26 Skill based Lab Course Networking with Linux Lab
  • MCAP21 Mini Project 1-B
  • MCA31 Big Data Analytics and Visualization
  • MCA32 Distributed System and Cloud Computing
  • MCAE33 Elective - 3
    • MCAE331 Blockchain
    • MCAE332 Deep Learning
    • MCAE333 Game Development
    • MCAE334 Ethical Hacking
    • MCAE335 Quantum Computing
  • MCAE34 Elective - 4
    • MCAE341 Intellectual Property Rights
    • MCAE342 Green Computing
    • MCAE343 Management Information System
    • MCAE344 Cyber Security and Digital Forensics
    • MCAE345 Entrepreneurship Management
  • MCAL31 Big Data Analytics and Visualization Lab
  • MCAL32 Distributed System and Cloud Computing Lab
  • MCALE33 Elective 3 Lab
  • MCAL34 Skill based Lab Mobile Computing Lab
  • MCAL35 Software Testing Quality Assurance Lab
  • MCAP31 Mini Project: 2 A
  • MCAI41 Internship
  • MCAR42 Research Paper
  • MCAM43 Online Course - (MOOC)
  • MCAS44 Institute Social Responsibility

PO 6 Professional Ethics: Understand and commit to professional ethics and cyber regulations, responsibilities, and norms of professional computing practices PO 7 Life-long Learning: Recognize the need and engage in lifelong learning independently for continual development to improve knowledge and competence as a computing professional PO 8 Project management and finance: An understanding of IT and management principles and apply to multidisciplinary software development, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects considering economical and financial factors. PO 9 Communication Efficacy: Understand and communicate effectively with the computing audiences and with society at large, about complex computing systems activities confidently and effectively by writing effective reports and design documentations. PO 10 Societal and Environmental Concern: Understand and assess societal, environmental, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues within local and global contexts, and the consequential responsibilities relevant to professional computing practices. PO 11 Individual and Team Work: Function effectively as an individual, as a member or leader in diverse teams in multidisciplinary environments. PO 12 Innovation and Entrepreneurship: Recognize timely opportunity and innovate to be an entrepreneur to pursue and create value addition for the betterment of the individual and society at large. PSO 1 Provide effective and efficient real time solutions using acquired knowledge in various domains. PSO 2 Apply standard practices of software project development for desktop, web, mobile and cross platform applications using modern tools and technologies, encouraging open-source programming environments.

pages.html:
Placements

Vision

To become a center of student professionals and entrepreneurs, who can meet out the corporate IT expectations and contribute for the betterment of society with new innovations.

Mission

To partner with IT industries, where the industries can provide the best opportunities to the students.

Goal

To provide the best opportunities to the students in IT industries for their best possible career path.

MCA Brouchure
Training & Placement Cell

The Training & Placement Cell facilitates the process of internship and placement of students passing out from the Institute besides collaborating with leading organizations.

The technology is changing the world & it becomes a challenging task to cater the needs of the industry, but we are proud to say that BVIMIT has faced the challenge successfully. This is possible due to outstanding efforts of the faculty and of the bright students who are selected after a stringent process. Our alumni have achieved tremendous success in all spheres and this bears an eloquent testimony to our efforts. The intelligence, high energy and motivation of our students, as well as their challenging work environments ensure that they bring an added measure of maturity and knowledge to the classroom.

BVIMIT is an exciting place to study and grow. It is a place where the thirst for knowledge fuels our desire for distinction. We are confident that these young students would be an asset to your organization through their technical and managerial capabilities and their talent for innovation. Our aim is to actively assist you in identifying the individuals best suited to your needs and in developing a successfully recruitment relationship.

We take pride in cordially inviting you to participate in our endeavor and look forward to welcoming you to the recruitment program. Providing link to enthusistics talents ready to explore the new horizon to meet the the requirement of corporate world

“WE SHAPE THOSE WHO SHAPE THE WORLD”

Pre-Placement Activities

Aptitude Test

Aptitude tests are regularly conducted by the institute to test the technical and logical reasoning skills of the students. This helps the students to measure their areas of improvement in a more précised way which would help them to get familiar to the various types of questions that can be asked during the competitive tests and interview.

Mock Interview

Mock interview is a practice that includes training for the students for the main interview sessions. The idea behind such practice is to make the students more comfortable with such an environment. This helps in the development of the skill set of the students.

Corporate Interface

Seminars and workshops are an integral part of the teaching pedagogy of BVIMIT to change the skill set of students. As a part of this a seminar was arranged for the Final Year MCA students of BVIMIT. The seminar focused on the development of interview skills in the students and also impart information to them about the current recruitment trends and expectations of the industry.

Placement Faculty

Technical Sessions/ Aptitude session Mock Interview/ Corporate Interface

Guest Session on Cloud Computing & AWS Technologies

We're honored to host Mr. Sanjay Sinha, Retired Deputy Director General from the National Informatics Centre, for an exclusive technical session on Cloud Computing & Amazon Web Services (AWS). With his extensive experience in e-Governance and cloud infrastructure, Mr. Sinha has provided invaluable insights to our MCA II Year students. Date: 21st July 2025,Time: 3:00 PM to 4:45 PM, Venue: Lab 102 These sessions underscore our commitment to blending industry expertise with academic learning, preparing our students for the dynamic tech landscape.

Industry visit & Alumni Interaction by the faculties

Strengthening Industry-Academia Collaboration! Between 17th May and 25th May 2025, a dedicated team of professors from Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Institute of Management and Information Technology (BVIMIT), Navi Mumbai, visited several esteemed companies as part of our ongoing efforts to bridge the gap between education and industry. These visits were aimed at: Strengthening industry-institute relationships Understanding emerging technologies and corporate expectations Exploring internship and placement opportunities for our students Discussing collaborative research and skill development initiatives We extend our heartfelt thanks to all the organizations that warmly welcomed us and shared valuable insights into their work culture and innovation strategies. At BVIMIT, we believe in continuous engagement with industry leaders to ensure our students are future-ready! Name of Companies visited by the MCA Facilities 1.IDBI Intech 2.AVS 3.Systenics 4.Veracitz 5.Quality Kiosk 6.Sciative 7.ICRUX

Mock Interview by Alumnus

Mock Interview Session: Empowering our students with career readiness! Our mock interview event was a successful initiative to prepare them for the professional world. The sessions are arranged between 7th April to 28th April 2025 by Mrs Priyanka Sani Software Engineer IDBI, Prathamesh Kharathe Software Engineer IDBI Intech, Satyam Pandey Software Engineer IDBI, Amith Asoken CEO Unipolar, Alumnus of Bharati Vidyapeeth’s IMIT,

Value Added Sessions (JAVA , DATABASE , Aptitude )

Technical Session by Siddesh Vattak & S N Nair (30 Hours) Mr. S. Vattak & Mr. S N Nair conducted a focused 30-hour Database & Java training under BVIMIT’s Placement Department. He delivered a hands-on sessions covering core Java and its practical applications: Core Concepts: Classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, and abstraction Hands-On Learning: Real-world tasks in Java programming, file I/O, and basic data manipulation Problem Solving Emphasis: Interactive code development to build confidence for technical rounds Impact: MCA final year students learned a solid understanding of Java, OOP fundamentals and practical coding skills, enhancing their readiness for software engineering roles. Aptitude Sessions by Rama Ma’am (17 Hours) 6th June onwards, Rama Ma’am conducted 17-hour of aptitude training series designed to strengthen students’ logic and quantitative skills: Topics Covered: Arithmetic, percentages, ratios, averages, data interpretation, and verbal reasoning Interactive Learning: Mock tests, time-based drills, and group problem-solving Techniques Taught: Shortcut methods, elimination strategies, and efficient time management in test scenarios Impact: MCA final year students significantly improve their speed and accuracy, building confidence for placement aptitude test.

Group Discussion

PDP Session gave students a platform to understand their personality and overcame the inhabitation of communication amongst their fellow class fellows before they step into a professional environment. Identify different personality traits and body language while communication Address the various effective way of greeting and strike a communication in a professional sphere Students divided into teams and were asked to participate in a group discussion. Students present are able to enhance their communication. Students are able to develop self-esteem and self-awareness, boosting students' confidence in themselves and their abilities Boost Presentation and Public Speaking Skills. Students are made to develop Professional Etiquette Name of the Trainer : Keval Gava Date : 05th October 2024

DBMS SESSION

BVIMIT has partnered with Techfer Global to conduct a session on SQL, aimed at empowering individuals and students to make the next generation of data engineers. Mr. Siddhesh Vartak, Head of Technology at Techfer Global, and Mr. Devendra Pimputkar , CEO at Techfer Global, conducted the session. Through this collaboration, students will gain cutting-edge skills and practical experience, preparing them for success in the rapidly evolving field of data engineering.

JAVA SESSION

BVIMIT offers a comprehensive 20-hour Java course designed to equip individuals and students with essential skills for a career in technology. The course is led by Mr. Sharath Nair, Program Manager for Technology Delivery and Support at AML and Innovation. This collaboration will provide students with in-depth knowledge and practical experience, preparing them for success in the dynamic field of software development.

AI/ML Session
BVIMIT and BVIMSR are excited to announce a new partnership with Zodiac HR Consultants India Pvt. Ltd. This collaboration includes an exclusive AI and ML workshop for MCA students. The MOU ceremony, attended by Mr. Preetam Sanil highlights this important alliance aimed at enhancing career development opportunities for students. The workshop, led by Ms. Vani Venkat, promises to provide valuable insights and hands-on experience in artificial intelligence and machine learning.
TECHNICAL TEST

Technical tests are conducted before placement drives to assess your practical skills and knowledge. These tests help Students understand how well they can apply what they've learned in the Classroom. They also ensure that you have the technical abilities needed for the job and can handle the tasks you'll face in your role. By performing well in these tests, you demonstrate that you're ready for the challenges of the job and increase your chances of getting hired. We help our students turn their classroom learning into real-world skills with special pre-placement sessions. These sessions include hands-on workshops, coding challenges, and industry-related projects designed to improve your technical skills. Whether you're a new engineer or a tech enthusiast, these activities give you a chance to show off your abilities and stand out in the job market. Get ready to unlock your potential and start a successful career with us.

APTITUDE SESSION

Our online aptitude session covers key math skills: calculating simple and compound interest, understanding profit and loss, finding averages, and solving ratio, proportion, and percentage problems. These essential skills are designed to enhance your problem-solving abilities and boost your confidence for job placements and competitive exams.

APTITUDE TEST

Our online aptitude session covers key math skills: calculating simple and compound interest, understanding profit and loss, finding averages, and solving ratio, proportion, and percentage problems. These essential skills are designed to enhance your problem-solving abilities and boost your confidence for job placements and competitive exams.

MOCK INTERVIEW

Get ready for your career with our pre-placement aptitude rounds, where you'll develop strong logic and problem-solving skills essential for landing a job. These sessions will help you tackle challenging problems, sharpen your thinking, and build confidence for interviews. BVIMIT alumni, who have achieved success in their careers, conduct the mock interviews, providing valuable insights and guidance. Among them are Hiten Maheshwari, Software Engineer at Adapty; Lijo Zachariah, Senior Quality Analyst at Corra; Amith Ashokan, Director at Unipolar Technologies; Suryaprakash Mishra, Senior Software Engineer at ICICI Prudential Asset Management; and Sushmita Bhor, Software Engineer at Pegasus Infocorp

RESUME BUILDING

BVIMIT recently hosted an insightful session led by Mr. Naresh Sonpal, Co-Creator and Chief Growth Officer at Next Leap Analytic Limited, along with his team. Mr. Sonpal expertly guided us through the complexities of resume building using AI technology. The session equipped us with the skills to create resumes that meet industry standards, significantly enhancing our professional development journey

SOFT SKILLS SESSION BY CAPTAIN DEVESH KUMAR

The event started with Captain Devesh Kumar's powerful speech, emphasising how important soft skills are in today's competitive world. He shared valuable knowledge on leadership, teamwork, and how to communicate effectively. Freelancer who has expertise in Neuro Linguistic Programming Ex-Merchant Navy Office.

SKILL DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM BY Mrs.AARTI AGARWAL

Mrs. Aarati Agrawal, Director of Vocskills, kept the energy high with an engaging session on the Skill Development Program. Her expertise in shaping professional careers inspired our students to commit to continuous learning and personal growth throughout their academic journey.

SIGNING MOU INDUSTRAIL INTERACTION

BVIMIT has signed over 25 Memorandums of Understanding with various companies. These agreements aim to give students valuable opportunities, such as working on live projects, attending seminars and workshops for training, and securing internships.

YOUTH CONCLAVE AAROHAN

Aarohan. Alok Sir's generous gesture of gifting the book "The Real Joy of Entertainment" added to the enriching experience. This involvement in Aarohan fills us with pride and reaffirms our commitment to engaging in meaningful platforms for holistic growth and learning. Additionally, the conclave hosts exhibitions, fostering an environment conducive to learning and growth. BVIMIT students cherish their involvement in Aarohan, recognizing it as a step towards India's cultural and spiritual advancement on the global stage.

Our alumni have achieved tremendous success in all spheres and this bears an eloquent testimony to our efforts. The intelligence, high energy and motivation of our students, as well as their challenging work environments ensure that they bring an added measure of maturity and knowledge to the classroom. BVIMIT is an exciting place to study and grow. It is a place where the thirst for knowledge fuels our desire for distinction. We are confident that these young students would be an asset to your organization through their technical and managerial capabilities and their talent for innovation. Our aim is to actively assist you in identifying the individuals best suited to your needs and in developing a successfully recruitment relationship. We take pride in cordially inviting you to participate in our endeavor and look forward to welcoming you to the recruitment program. blocks.heading: Pre-Placement Activities
blocks.text: Pre-Placement Activities Aptitude Test Aptitude tests are regularly conducted by the institute to test the technical and logical reasoning skills of the students. This helps the students to measure their areas of improvement in a more précised way which would help them to get familiar to the various types of questions that can be asked during the competitive tests and interview. Mock Interview Mock interview is a practice that includes training for the students for the main interview sessions. The idea behind such practice is to make the students more comfortable with such an environment. This helps in the development of the skill set of the students. Corporate Interface Seminars and workshops are an integral part of the teaching pedagogy of BVIMIT to change the skill set of students. As a part of this a seminar was arranged for the Final Year MCA students of BVIMIT. The seminar focused on the development of interview skills in the students and also impart information to them about the current recruitment trends and expectations of the industry.

blocks.heading: Technical Sessions/ Aptitude session Mock Interview/ Corporate Interface
blocks.text: Technical Sessions/ Aptitude session Mock Interview/ Corporate Interface Guest Session on Cloud Computing & AWS Technologies We're honored to host Mr. Sanjay Sinha, Retired Deputy Director General from the National Informatics Centre, for an exclusive technical session on Cloud Computing & Amazon Web Services (AWS). With his extensive experience in e-Governance and cloud infrastructure, Mr. Sinha has provided invaluable insights to our MCA II Year students. Date: 21st July 2025,Time: 3:00 PM to 4:45 PM, Venue: Lab 102 These sessions underscore our commitment to blending industry expertise with academic learning, preparing our students for the dynamic tech landscape. Industry visit & Alumni Interaction by the faculties Strengthening Industry-Academia Collaboration! Between 17th May and 25th May 2025, a dedicated team of professors from Bharati Vidyapeeth’s Institute of Management and Information Technology (BVIMIT), Navi Mumbai, visited several esteemed companies as part of our ongoing efforts to bridge the gap between education and industry. These visits were aimed at: Strengthening industry-institute relationships Understanding emerging technologies and corporate expectations Exploring internship and placement opportunities for our students Discussing collaborative research and skill development initiatives We extend our heartfelt thanks to all the organizations that warmly welcomed us and shared valuable insights into their work culture and innovation strategies. At BVIMIT, we believe in continuous engagement with industry leaders to ensure our students are future-ready! Name of Companies visited by the MCA Facilities 1.IDBI Intech 2.AVS 3.Systenics 4.Veracitz 5.Quality Kiosk 6.Sciative 7.ICRUX Mock Interview by Alumnus Mock Interview Session: Empowering our students with career readiness! Our mock interview event was a successful initiative to prepare them for the professional world. The sessions are arranged between 7th April to 28th April 2025 by Mrs Priyanka Sani Software Engineer IDBI, Prathamesh Kharathe Software Engineer IDBI Intech, Satyam Pandey Software Engineer IDBI, Amith Asoken CEO Unipolar, Alumnus of Bharati Vidyapeeth’s IMIT, Value Added Sessions (JAVA , DATABASE , Aptitude ) Technical Session by Siddesh Vattak & S N Nair (30 Hours) Mr. S. Vattak & Mr. S N Nair conducted a focused 30-hour Database & Java training under BVIMIT’s Placement Department. He delivered a hands-on sessions covering core Java and its practical applications: Core Concepts: Classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, and abstraction Hands-On Learning: Real-world tasks in Java programming, file I/O, and basic data manipulation Problem Solving Emphasis: Interactive code development to build confidence for technical rounds Impact: MCA final year students learned a solid understanding of Java, OOP fundamentals and practical coding skills, enhancing their readiness for software engineering roles. Aptitude Sessions by Rama Ma’am (17 Hours) 6th June onwards, Rama Ma’am conducted 17-hour of aptitude training series designed to strengthen students’ logic and quantitative skills: Topics Covered: Arithmetic, percentages, ratios, averages, data interpretation, and verbal reasoning Interactive Learning: Mock tests, time-based drills, and group problem-solving Techniques Taught: Shortcut methods, elimination strategies, and efficient time management in test scenarios Impact: MCA final year students significantly improve their speed and accuracy, building confidence for placement aptitude test. Group Discussion PDP Session gave students a platform to understand their personality and overcame the inhabitation of communication amongst their fellow class fellows before they step into a professional environment. Identify different personality traits and body language while communication Address the various effective way of greeting and strike a communication in a professional sphere Students divided into teams and were asked to participate in a group discussion. Students present are able to enhance their communication. Students are able to develop self-esteem and self-awareness, boosting students' confidence in themselves and their abilities Boost Presentation and Public Speaking Skills. Students are made to develop Professional Etiquette Name of the Trainer : Keval Gava Date : 05th October 2024 DBMS SESSION BVIMIT has partnered with Techfer Global to conduct a session on SQL, aimed at empowering individuals and students to make the next generation of data engineers. Mr. Siddhesh Vartak, Head of Technology at Techfer Global, and Mr. Devendra Pimputkar , CEO at Techfer Global, conducted the session. Through this collaboration, students will gain cutting-edge skills and practical experience, preparing them for success in the rapidly evolving field of data engineering. JAVA SESSION BVIMIT offers a comprehensive 20-hour Java course designed to equip individuals and students with essential skills for a career in technology. The course is led by Mr. Sharath Nair, Program Manager for Technology Delivery and Support at AML and Innovation. This collaboration will provide students with in-depth knowledge and practical experience, preparing them for success in the dynamic field of software development. AI/ML Session BVIMIT and BVIMSR are excited to announce a new partnership with Zodiac HR Consultants India Pvt. Ltd. This collaboration includes an exclusive AI and ML workshop for MCA students. The MOU ceremony, attended by Mr. Preetam Sanil highlights this important alliance aimed at enhancing career development opportunities for students. The workshop, led by Ms. Vani Venkat, promises to provide valuable insights and hands-on experience in artificial intelligence and machine learning. TECHNICAL TEST Technical tests are conducted before placement drives to assess your practical skills and knowledge. These tests help Students understand how well they can apply what they've learned in the Classroom. They also ensure that you have the technical abilities needed for the job and can handle the tasks you'll face in your role. By performing well in these tests, you demonstrate that you're ready for the challenges of the job and increase your chances of getting hired. We help our students turn their classroom learning into real-world skills with special pre-placement sessions. These sessions include hands-on workshops, coding challenges, and industry-related projects designed to improve your technical skills. Whether you're a new engineer or a tech enthusiast, these activities give you a chance to show off your abilities and stand out in the job market. Get ready to unlock your potential and start a successful career with us. APTITUDE SESSION Our online aptitude session covers key math skills: calculating simple and compound interest, understanding profit and loss, finding averages, and solving ratio, proportion, and percentage problems. These essential skills are designed to enhance your problem-solving abilities and boost your confidence for job placements and competitive exams. APTITUDE TEST Our online aptitude session covers key math skills: calculating simple and compound interest, understanding profit and loss, finding averages, and solving ratio, proportion, and percentage problems. These essential skills are designed to enhance your problem-solving abilities and boost your confidence for job placements and competitive exams. MOCK INTERVIEW Get ready for your career with our pre-placement aptitude rounds, where you'll develop strong logic and problem-solving skills essential for landing a job. These sessions will help you tackle challenging problems, sharpen your thinking, and build confidence for interviews. BVIMIT alumni, who have achieved success in their careers, conduct the mock interviews, providing valuable insights and guidance. Among them are Hiten Maheshwari, Software Engineer at Adapty; Lijo Zachariah, Senior Quality Analyst at Corra; Amith Ashokan, Director at Unipolar Technologies; Suryaprakash Mishra, Senior Software Engineer at ICICI Prudential Asset Management; and Sushmita Bhor, Software Engineer at Pegasus Infocorp RESUME BUILDING BVIMIT recently hosted an insightful session led by Mr. Naresh Sonpal, Co-Creator and Chief Growth Officer at Next Leap Analytic Limited, along with his team. Mr. Sonpal expertly guided us through the complexities of resume building using AI technology. The session equipped us with the skills to create resumes that meet industry standards, significantly enhancing our professional development journey SOFT SKILLS SESSION BY CAPTAIN DEVESH KUMAR The event started with Captain Devesh Kumar's powerful speech, emphasising how important soft skills are in today's competitive world. He shared valuable knowledge on leadership, teamwork, and how to communicate effectively. Freelancer who has expertise in Neuro Linguistic Programming Ex-Merchant Navy Office. SKILL DEVELOPMENT PROGRAM BY Mrs.AARTI AGARWAL Mrs. Aarati Agrawal, Director of Vocskills, kept the energy high with an engaging session on the Skill Development Program. Her expertise in shaping professional careers inspired our students to commit to continuous learning and personal growth throughout their academic journey. SIGNING MOU INDUSTRAIL INTERACTION BVIMIT has signed over 25 Memorandums of Understanding with various companies. These agreements aim to give students valuable opportunities, such as working on live projects, attending seminars and workshops for training, and securing internships. YOUTH CONCLAVE AAROHAN Aarohan. Alok Sir's generous gesture of gifting the book "The Real Joy of Entertainment" added to the enriching experience. This involvement in Aarohan fills us with pride and reaffirms our commitment to engaging in meaningful platforms for holistic growth and learning. Additionally, the conclave hosts exhibitions, fostering an environment conducive to learning and growth. BVIMIT students cherish their involvement in Aarohan, recognizing it as a step towards India's cultural and spiritual advancement on the global stage.
